title: A2: Delete intent-ledger compliance regime
description: Remove intent-ledger compliance regime (zero adoption: 31/31 audit.jsonl entries have matched_get_rules_ts=null). Delete services/annotate-intent.ts, services/human-lock-or-equivalent.ts, services/audit-log.ts, api/intent.ts, api/events.ts forensic-loading code path, .fabric/audit.jsonl, .fabric/forensic.json. Keep serve-lock, in-flight-tracker, rehydrate-state, read-ledger (server lifecycle infra, not compliance). Audit read-ledger callers; if only consumed by deleted modules, delete it too. Anchors: handoff.json#code_anchors not specific to A2 but cross-reference exploration-codebase.json#module_map.events_ledger.

files.change: Strip forensic.json loading code path; events.jsonl read-only stays
grep call sites; if only deleted modules ref it, delete; otherwise keep

implementation: Grep for imports of annotate-intent / human-lock-or-equivalent / audit-log / api/intent across packages/server/src
Update http.ts to drop intent route registration
Strip forensic-loading branch in api/events.ts (keep events.jsonl-only path)
Audit read-ledger.ts: grep usages; if 0 surviving callers, delete; else keep
Verify build (pnpm -w build) and existing tests still pass

reference.examples: Follow existing route unmount pattern; preserve serve-lock/in-flight-tracker/rehydrate-state imports

criteria: Deleted from disk: services/annotate-intent.ts, services/human-lock-or-equivalent.ts, services/audit-log.ts, api/intent.ts, .fabric/audit.jsonl, .fabric/forensic.json
Kept on disk: services/serve-lock.ts, services/in-flight-tracker.ts, services/rehydrate-state.ts, services/read-ledger.ts (verified still used)
pnpm -w build succeeds with zero compile errors related to intent/audit/forensic

rationale.chosen_approach: Wholesale delete; no migration path needed (zero adopters per audit.jsonl evidence)
rationale.tradeoffs: Lose ability to reactivate compliance regime without resurrecting code; acceptable given pivot direction

manual_checks: Boot server, verify it still starts

risks.description: read-ledger.ts has surviving callers we miss
risks.mitigation: grep cross-package before deletion; defer to keep if any non-deleted caller
